title: Lasianthus neolanceolatus
intro: Lasianthus neolanceolatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae, commonly known as the lance-leaf Lasianthus. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lasianthus neolanceolatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, often in combination with other herbal remedies.

properties: Lasianthus neolanceolatus is reported to have various therapeutic properties. It is traditionally used in traditional medicine for its potential antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Its extracts have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity, which may help in wound healing and preventing infections. Furthermore, the plant's antioxidant properties are believed to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic potential and effects on human health.
